steps.title: Identify the relationship between ideas
steps.content: Italian was the standard language for opera for centuries; now singers encounter Italian terms in old scores. The logical relationship is cause-effect: historical dominance explains present experience.

steps.title: Match the transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship is cause-effect. Italian dominated opera for centuries; as a direct consequence, singers today encounter Italian terms in old scores.
  • Choice A — Consequently,: signals that the second sentence is a result of the first — historical Italian dominance explains present-day encounters, so fits. ✓
  • Choice B — However,: signals an unexpected reversal, implying singers today are NOT encountering Italian — contradicts the passage, so fails. ✗
  • Choice C — Lastly,: signals the final item in a listed series — no list is present, so fails. ✗
  • Choice D — Granted,: signals a concession about to be countered, but no counterargument follows, so fails. ✗
A is correct.
